palette_hint: Deep charcoal background #1a1a1e with off-white text #f0ede6, electric amber accent #f5a623, and secondary violet accent #7b5ea7.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif, bold weight at large scale (3–6rem) for editorial tension; body in system-ui at 1rem/1.6 line-height for clarity; feels like a printed design annual.
description: Obsidian Fold is a boutique brand-identity studio whose website uses non-rectangular geometry, SVG-drawn logomarks, and clipped compositions to demonstrate the studio's obsession with precise, angular visual systems.
constraints: Every page section must be separated by a diagonal CSS clip-path cut (polygon slicing at roughly 4deg) rather than horizontal rules, using the charcoal and amber alternating panel colors.
The studio logomark — a folded origami-style octagon constructed entirely from inline SVG paths — must appear in the header on every page at 48×48px, drawn in amber #f5a623 on charcoal.
Content columns are max-width 1100px centered, with 64px horizontal padding; body text columns never exceed 680px for readability.

Accent SVG shapes (rotated rectangles, skewed parallelograms) must be layered as decorative backgrounds behind section headings using CSS transform: rotate() and skewX() combinations, colored at 15% opacity in #f5a623.
All 'case study' thumbnail cards on identity-work use clip-path: polygon() to cut their top-right corner at 40px, giving every card a chamfered feel consistent with the angular brand language.

page_specs.manifesto: A full-viewport hero panel with the headline 'Shape Before Color. Silence Before Noise.' set in Georgia bold at 5rem, overlaid on an inline SVG background of interlocking angular grid lines in #7b5ea7 at 20% opacity. Below the hero, two diagonal-cut panels present the studio's core beliefs in 3 short paragraphs each: 'On Identity', 'On Craft', and 'On Restraint', each introduced by an SVG-drawn geometric icon (triangle, diamond, chevron) filled in amber.
page_specs.process: A vertically stacked, numbered four-step process — Discover, Distill, Draw, Deploy — where each step occupies an alternating charcoal/violet (#7b5ea7 at 12% tint) diagonal panel. Each step heading is 2.5rem Georgia bold with a large hairline SVG step-number watermark (stroke only, no fill) at 15rem behind it. A horizontal SVG timeline connector line with diamond waypoints links the steps along the left edge.
page_specs.identity-work: A grid of eight brand identity case studies laid out in a 3-column CSS grid with 24px gaps, each card clipped with a chamfered top-right corner via clip-path: polygon(). Each card shows the client name in amber, a one-line descriptor, and a purely SVG-constructed representative mark (wordmark silhouette or icon) specific to that client — clients include Halverson Ceramics, Rue Neuf Parfums, Kestrel Air Charter, and Duskline Records among others. Hovering a card shifts the clip-path via CSS transition to reveal a second chamfer on the bottom-left.
page_specs.typefaces: A showcase of the three custom type pairings the studio uses in client work — 'Anchor & Float', 'Blade & Reed', and 'Monolith & Script' — each demonstrated in a wide diagonal-cut panel. Each pairing is shown as an oversized SVG text-path specimen (letters following a curved SVG path), accompanied by specimen size scales from 8pt to 96pt rendered as inline HTML, and annotation arrows drawn as SVG polylines with arrowhead markers pointing to specific letterforms.
page_specs.contact: A single centered panel with a large inline SVG illustration of a folded envelope constructed from geometric polygons in charcoal and amber, above a minimal contact block listing the studio email, Berlin studio address, and new-project inquiry note. Below the illustration, three SVG-icon social links (each a custom-drawn path icon for Email, LinkedIn, and Are.na) sit in a row. The panel background uses an SVG pattern of tiny repeating diamond tiles at 5% opacity as a subtle texture.
